select_when: Uncertainty is high, early commitment is expensive, and cheap experiments can buy learning before an irreversible call.
Decisions are reversible enough that preserving multiple paths creates information value rather than just indecision.
Downside can be capped while upside remains open across several plausible futures.
The problem has collapsed into binary framing, but the real move is to create more viable paths before choosing.
avoid_when: Optionality has become a permanent excuse not to commit even after evidence has clarified which path has the best economics or strategic fit.
Maintaining optionality is consuming cash, attention, or organizational simplicity that would be better spent on a chosen path.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on preserving viable paths under uncertainty rather than on generic flexibility rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style portfolio or investing overlays because the raw source is stronger on reversible decisions, option generation, and premature-commitment resistance.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stops at the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The input and output labels are reviewed operational compression over a source that mixes strategic, cognitive, and negotiation examples.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on high uncertainty, reversibility, and downside-capped upside-open situations; review mainly compacts those bullets into routing form.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by explicit danger lines in the limitations section.

input_type.note: The source activates on uncertain decisions with reversible elements, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook explicitly creates and evaluates multiple paths; the output label compresses that into a typed expansion artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Optionality clearly uses counterfactual and probabilistic reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
